mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is a Python SDK and Proxy Server that allows users to call over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It is ideal for applications requiring AI capabilities with cost tracking and load balancing. Key trade-offs include the need for proper API key management and potential costs associated with API usage.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

pip install litellm==1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch6

```

> Install the LiteLLM SDK.

steps.title: Create the Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a new Kubernetes cluster in your preferred cloud provider with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Deploy LiteLLM Proxy Server
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM Proxy Server using Helm to manage the installation.

```bash

helm repo add litellm https://berriai.github.io/litellm

helm install litellm litellm/litellm --version 1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch6 --namespace litellm --create-namespace

```

> Add the LiteLLM Helm repository.
> Install LiteLLM in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace litellm

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
This will remove the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that any data stored in the cluster may be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall litellm --namespace litellm

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the LiteLLM Helm release.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. After upgrading, verify that the deployment is functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get deployment litellm -n litellm -o yaml > litellm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade litellm litellm/litellm --version 1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch6 --namespace litellm

kubectl get pods -n litellm

```

> Backup the current deployment configuration.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that the pods are running correctly after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get deployment litellm -n litellm -o yaml > litellm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region us-central1' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated to handle the current load.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or adjust the resource requests in your deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are issues with the application configuration or dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n litellm' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that Kubernetes cannot pull the container image, possibly due to incorrect image name or tag.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLM APIs and improved load balancing features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the legacy API endpoints for LLM calls.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your API calls to use the new endpoints as specified in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n litellm' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
